project.name: abilities-mcp-adapter
project.description: Abilities MCP Adapter — exposes WordPress abilities as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, resources, and prompts.

nodes.name: HandlerHelperTrait.php
nodes.summary: Shared trait giving MCP handlers helpers for extracting params, building success/error responses, and producing standard error responses via McpErrorFactory.

nodes.name: InitializeHandler.php
nodes.summary: Handles the MCP initialize request, negotiating protocol version and returning server capabilities and identity metadata.

nodes.name: InitializeHandler
nodes.summary: Responds to MCP initialize by negotiating the protocol version and reporting server name, version, and description.

nodes.name: handle
nodes.summary: Negotiates the requested protocol version and returns the server's capabilities and identity for the initialize response.

nodes.name: PromptsHandler.php
nodes.summary: MCP handler for prompts/list and prompts/get, enforcing OAuth scope and ability permissions before executing the prompt's backing ability and shaping the result.

nodes.name: PromptsHandler
nodes.summary: Lists registered prompts and resolves a single prompt, running permission and scope checks then executing the prompt or its ability.

nodes.name: get_prompt
nodes.summary: Resolves a prompt by name, enforces permission and OAuth scope, executes the backing ability or builder, and returns prompt messages or an error.

nodes.name: list_prompts
nodes.summary: Returns the array of registered prompts serialized for the prompts/list response.

nodes.name: ResourcesHandler.php
nodes.summary: MCP handler for resources/list and resources/read that enforces OAuth scope and ability permissions before resolving a resource's content via its backing ability.

nodes.name: ResourcesHandler
nodes.summary: Lists registered resources and reads a single resource, running permission and scope checks before executing the resource's ability to produce content.

nodes.name: read_resource
nodes.summary: Resolves a resource by URI, enforces permission and OAuth scope, executes the backing ability, and returns the resource contents or an error.

nodes.name: list_resources
nodes.summary: Returns the array of registered resources serialized for the resources/list response.

nodes.name: SystemHandler.php
nodes.summary: Handles MCP system/protocol methods (ping, logging level, completion, roots listing) and produces method-not-found errors for unsupported requests.

nodes.name: SystemHandler
nodes.summary: Implements lightweight MCP system methods and the fallback method-not-found responder.

nodes.name: ToolsHandler.php
nodes.summary: MCP handler for tools/list and tools/call that sanitizes tool data, enforces permission/scope, executes the backing ability, and shapes the result into MCP tool content.

nodes.name: ToolsHandler
nodes.summary: Lists tools and handles tool calls end-to-end: validation, permission and OAuth scope enforcement, ability execution, and result/error formatting.

nodes.name: handle_tool_call
nodes.summary: Resolves a tool, checks PermissionManager state, ability permissions, and OAuth scope, executes the ability, and maps results or WP_Errors to MCP responses.

nodes.name: call_tool
nodes.summary: Entry point for tools/call: extracts the tool name and arguments, delegates to handle_tool_call, and normalizes the response content.

nodes.name: sanitize_tool_data
nodes.summary: Serializes a tool to array and strips internal-only fields before exposing it in tools/list responses.

nodes.name: list_tools
nodes.summary: Returns sanitized registered tools for the tools/list response.

nodes.name: McpErrorFactory.php
nodes.summary: Factory producing standardized JSON-RPC/MCP error responses for all error categories and mapping MCP error codes to HTTP statuses.

nodes.name: McpErrorFactory
nodes.summary: Static error factory with named constructors for each MCP error type, HTTP-status mapping, and JSON-RPC message validation.

nodes.name: create_error_response
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical JSON-RPC error envelope from an id, code, message, and optional data.

nodes.name: mcp_error_to_http_status
nodes.summary: Maps an MCP/JSON-RPC error code to the appropriate HTTP status code for transport responses.

nodes.name: validate_jsonrpc_message
nodes.summary: Validates that an incoming message conforms to JSON-RPC structure, returning an invalid_request error otherwise.

nodes.name: rate_limited
nodes.summary: Produces a rate-limit error response carrying retry-after, limit, window, and dimension details.

nodes.name: McpErrorMapper.php
nodes.summary: Maps WordPress WP_Error codes onto MCP error codes and converts a WP_Error into a standardized MCP error response via McpErrorFactory.

nodes.name: McpErrorMapper
nodes.summary: Static mapper translating WP_Error codes to MCP error codes and producing MCP error responses from WP_Errors.

nodes.name: from_wp_error
nodes.summary: Converts a WP_Error into an MCP error response, mapping its code and forwarding message and data.

nodes.name: McpTransportInterface.php
nodes.summary: Contract that MCP transport implementations must satisfy, declaring construction with a transport context and route registration.

nodes.name: McpTransportInterface
nodes.summary: Interface defining the constructor and register_routes contract for transports.

nodes.name: HttpSessionValidator.php
nodes.summary: Validates, creates, and terminates MCP HTTP sessions, delegating to SessionManager and returning MCP errors for unauthorized or invalid sessions.

nodes.name: HttpSessionValidator
nodes.summary: Provides session validation, header validation, creation, and termination for the HTTP transport using SessionManager.

nodes.name: validate_session
nodes.summary: Validates an MCP session for the request, checking the logged-in user and verifying the session token via SessionManager.

nodes.name: McpTransportContext.php
nodes.summary: Immutable context object bundling the MCP server, all request handlers, observability/error handlers, request router, and permission callback for transports.

nodes.name: McpTransportContext
nodes.summary: Holds the wired-together handlers and dependencies a transport needs, populated from a properties array in its constructor.

nodes.name: OutputSchemaPassthroughTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying McpTool serializes its output schema into tool data, omits it when null, and validates it via McpToolValidator.

nodes.name: OutputSchemaPassthroughTest
nodes.summary: Test case asserting output-schema passthrough behavior of McpTool's to_array and validation.

nodes.name: McpAnnotationMapperTest.php
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for McpAnnotationMapper::map covering per-feature-type filtering, value casting, exclusion of empty/null values, and field overrides.

nodes.name: McpAnnotationMapperTest
nodes.summary: Test case exercising annotation mapping and normalization rules for tools, resources, and prompts.

nodes.name: SchemaTransformerTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for SchemaTransformer covering minimal object output, object passthrough, and wrapping of scalar/array/typed schemas under a wrapper key.

nodes.name: SchemaTransformerTest
nodes.summary: Test case validating SchemaTransformer::transform_to_object_schema across schema shapes.

nodes.name: HandlerHelperTraitTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for HandlerHelperTrait via a consumer stub, covering param extraction, error/success response formatting, and factory-response unwrapping.

nodes.name: HandlerHelperTraitTest
nodes.summary: Test case exercising the handler helper trait's param extraction and response helpers.

nodes.name: PromptsHandlerScopeEnforcementTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying PromptsHandler enforces OAuth scope on prompts/get using the OAuthRequestContext and rejects out-of-scope requests.

nodes.name: PromptsHandlerScopeEnforcementTest
nodes.summary: Test case asserting OAuth scope enforcement behavior of the PromptsHandler.

nodes.name: ResourcesHandlerScopeEnforcementTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying ResourcesHandler enforces OAuth scope on resources/read via the OAuthRequestContext.

nodes.name: ResourcesHandlerScopeEnforcementTest
nodes.summary: Test case asserting OAuth scope enforcement behavior of the ResourcesHandler.

nodes.name: PermissionErrorRemediationTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests checking that permission-denied errors from tool execution carry remediation guidance, exercising meta-abilities and the error factory/mapper.

nodes.name: PermissionErrorRemediationTest
nodes.summary: Test case asserting permission errors include remediation hints across abilities and error mapping.

nodes.name: McpErrorFactoryTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for McpErrorFactory covering each error constructor, HTTP-status mapping, and JSON-RPC message validation.

nodes.name: McpErrorFactoryTest
nodes.summary: Test case exercising the full surface of the MCP error factory.

nodes.name: McpErrorMapperTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for McpErrorMapper verifying WP_Error code mapping and conversion into MCP error responses.

nodes.name: McpErrorMapperTest
nodes.summary: Test case validating WP_Error-to-MCP error mapping.

nodes.name: GetAbilityInfoAbility.php
nodes.summary: Meta-ability that registers a WordPress ability returning metadata (name, label, schema, meta) about another registered ability, gated by permission and MCP-exposure checks.

nodes.name: GetAbilityInfoAbility
nodes.summary: Registers and executes the get-ability-info meta-ability, validating user access and returning a target ability's descriptor.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers the get-ability-info ability with WordPress, declaring its input schema, permission callback, and execute callback.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Looks up the requested ability and returns a structured array of its name, label, description, category, schemas, and meta.

nodes.name: validate_user_access
nodes.summary: Verifies the current user is logged in and holds the capability required to introspect abilities.

nodes.name: McpComponentRegistry.php
nodes.summary: Central registry that converts WordPress abilities into MCP tools, resources, and prompts, validating each and recording observability events on success and failure.

nodes.name: McpComponentRegistry
nodes.summary: Builds and stores MCP tool/resource/prompt instances from ability names, delegating creation to Register* factories and validation to domain validators.

nodes.name: register_tools
nodes.summary: Resolves each ability name into an MCP tool via RegisterAbilityAsMcpTool, validating and recording success/failure events.

nodes.name: add_tool
nodes.summary: Adds a pre-built McpTool instance to the registry after binding the server and validating the tool.

nodes.name: register_resources
nodes.summary: Converts ability names into MCP resources via RegisterAbilityAsMcpResource, recording registration outcomes.

nodes.name: register_prompts
nodes.summary: Registers prompts from either prompt-builder classes or ability names, validating each prompt instance before storing it.

nodes.name: McpServer.php
nodes.summary: Core MCP server aggregate that holds server identity/config, wires up the component registry and transport factory, and exposes accessors for tools, resources, prompts, and handlers.

nodes.name: McpServer
nodes.summary: Represents a configured MCP server: stores identity and route metadata, initializes handlers/components/transports, and provides getters used throughout the request pipeline.

nodes.name: __construct
nodes.summary: Builds the server from identity, route, handler, and component arguments, then triggers handler and component setup.

nodes.name: setup_components
nodes.summary: Registers MCP components into the registry and initializes the configured transports via the transport factory.

nodes.name: register_mcp_components
nodes.summary: Delegates tool, resource, and prompt registration to the component registry when each collection is non-empty.

nodes.name: McpTransportFactory.php
nodes.summary: Factory that validates and initializes configured transport classes against the MCP transport interface and assembles the shared transport context with all request handlers.

nodes.name: McpTransportFactory
nodes.summary: Instantiates transport implementations for an MCP server and creates the transport context wiring handlers, observability, and the permission callback.

nodes.name: initialize_transports
nodes.summary: Iterates configured transport classes, verifying each exists and implements McpTransportInterface before constructing it with the transport context.

nodes.name: create_transport_context
nodes.summary: Assembles an McpTransportContext from the server's handlers, observability handler, error handler, and permission callback.

nodes.name: McpPromptBuilderInterface.php
nodes.summary: Contract for fluent prompt builders defining the build, metadata accessor, handle, and permission methods an MCP prompt builder must implement.

nodes.name: McpPromptBuilderInterface
nodes.summary: Interface declaring the build/handle/permission surface for prompt builder implementations.

nodes.name: McpPrompt.php
nodes.summary: Domain value object representing an MCP prompt with arguments and annotations, supporting serialization, validation, and optional builder-based direct execution.

nodes.name: McpPrompt
nodes.summary: Encapsulates a prompt's name, title, description, arguments, and annotations with getters/setters, to_array/to_json, from_array hydration, and validation against the server.

nodes.name: to_array
nodes.summary: Serializes the prompt to an associative array, omitting null/empty optional fields.

nodes.name: from_array
nodes.summary: Hydrates an McpPrompt from array data, binds the MCP server, and validates the resulting instance.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validates the prompt instance against the server when MCP validation is enabled, returning a WP_Error on failure.

nodes.name: get_ability
nodes.summary: Resolves and returns the backing WordPress ability for this prompt, erroring if it is not registered.

nodes.name: McpPromptBuilder.php
nodes.summary: Abstract fluent base class for code-defined prompts, providing argument/annotation helpers and lazily configuring metadata via a subclass configure() hook.

nodes.name: McpPromptBuilder
nodes.summary: Base builder implementing McpPromptBuilderInterface with lazy configuration and argument construction helpers for subclassed prompts.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Runs configure() and assembles the prompt definition array from the builder's accumulated name, title, description, arguments, and annotations.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpPrompt.php
nodes.summary: Adapter that derives an MCP prompt definition from a WordPress ability, converting its input schema into prompt arguments and mapping annotations.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds an McpPrompt from an ability, exposing a static make() factory and input-schema-to-arguments conversion.

nodes.name: get_data
nodes.summary: Derives the prompt's name, title, description, arguments, and annotations from the backing ability's metadata.

nodes.name: convert_input_schema_to_arguments
nodes.summary: Translates a JSON-schema properties object into a list of MCP prompt argument descriptors with required flags.

nodes.name: McpResource.php
nodes.summary: Domain value object for an MCP resource (text or blob) with URI, mime type, and annotations, supporting serialization, hydration, and validation.

nodes.name: McpResource
nodes.summary: Represents a resource exposed over MCP with text/blob content, type predicates, getters/setters, and to_array/from_array conversion plus validation.

nodes.name: to_array
nodes.summary: Serializes the resource to an array, including only the populated text or blob fields and non-empty annotations.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validates the resource instance against the server via McpResourceValidator when validation is enabled.

nodes.name: get_ability
nodes.summary: Resolves the backing WordPress ability for this resource, erroring when it is not registered.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpResource.php
nodes.summary: Adapter that builds an MCP resource definition from a WordPress ability, deriving its URI, descriptive metadata, and content.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpResource
nodes.summary: Derives an McpResource from an ability via a static make() factory, resolving URI, metadata, and ability content.

nodes.name: get_data
nodes.summary: Assembles the resource definition array (URI, name, description, annotations) from the backing ability.

nodes.name: McpTool.php
nodes.summary: Domain value object for an MCP tool carrying input/output schemas, annotations, and metadata, with serialization, hydration, and validation against the server.

nodes.name: McpTool
nodes.summary: Represents an MCP tool bound to a WordPress ability, exposing schema/annotation/metadata accessors, to_array/from_array, and validate().

nodes.name: to_array
nodes.summary: Serializes the tool to an array including name, schemas, and annotations, omitting null/empty optional fields.

nodes.name: validate
nodes.summary: Validates the tool instance via McpToolValidator when server-side MCP validation is enabled.

nodes.name: get_ability
nodes.summary: Resolves the backing WordPress ability for this tool, returning an error when it is not registered.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpTool.php
nodes.summary: Adapter that builds an MCP tool from a WordPress ability, transforming input/output schemas into object schemas and mapping annotations.

nodes.name: RegisterAbilityAsMcpTool
nodes.summary: Derives an McpTool from an ability via a static make() factory, using SchemaTransformer and McpAnnotationMapper.

nodes.name: get_data
nodes.summary: Builds the tool definition array, normalizing the ability's input/output schemas to object schemas and attaching annotations and metadata.

nodes.name: McpAnnotationMapper.php
nodes.summary: Maps WordPress ability meta and annotations into MCP feature annotations, applying per-feature-type filtering and type normalization.

nodes.name: McpAnnotationMapper
nodes.summary: Static mapper producing MCP annotations from ability annotations for tools, resources, and prompts with value resolution and normalization.

nodes.name: build_from_ability
nodes.summary: Reads an ability's meta to assemble candidate annotations (category, tier, permission) then maps them for the given feature type.

nodes.name: map
nodes.summary: Filters and resolves ability annotations against the MCP annotation schema for the requested feature type.

nodes.name: normalize_annotation_value
nodes.summary: Coerces an annotation value to its declared field type (boolean, string, array, number), excluding empty or non-conforming values.

nodes.name: SchemaTransformer.php
nodes.summary: Utility that normalizes arbitrary JSON schemas into object-typed schemas, wrapping non-object schemas under a configurable key for MCP compatibility.

nodes.name: SchemaTransformer
nodes.summary: Static helper that converts schemas to object schemas, wrapping scalar/array schemas under a wrapper key.

nodes.name: transform_to_object_schema
nodes.summary: Returns a minimal object schema for empty input, passes through object schemas, and wraps other typed schemas under the wrapper key.

nodes.name: McpTransportHelperTrait.php
nodes.summary: Shared trait providing helper methods reused across MCP transport classes.
nodes.languageNotes: PHP trait for horizontal code reuse across transport implementations (no class extracted).

nodes.name: OriginValidator.php
nodes.summary: Validates the HTTP Origin header against an allowlist (home URL plus loopback) to defend MCP routes against cross-site request forgery.

nodes.name: OriginValidator
nodes.summary: Checks request Origin against allowed hosts and loopback, returning whether it is permitted and the safe echoable origin for CORS.

nodes.name: RequestRouter.php
nodes.summary: Routes MCP JSON-RPC methods to handlers, applying rate limiting, cursor compatibility, session-aware initialize, and error categorization.

nodes.name: RequestRouter
nodes.summary: Dispatches MCP method calls, enforces rate limits via the limiter and trusted-proxy resolver, handles initialize/session, and categorizes/sanitizes errors for logging.

nodes.name: SettingsAbilitiesTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for SettingsAbilities verifying friction-gated guards, one-time tokens, param-swap rejection, and Bucket-1/2/3 keyword enforcement.

nodes.name: SettingsAbilitiesTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class exercising the redaction-settings abilities' guard logic and confirmation-token flow.

nodes.name: OAuthClientIpTrustedProxyTest.php

nodes.name: OAuthClientIpTrustedProxyTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating trusted-proxy-aware client IP extraction across allowlist and bypass scenarios.

nodes.name: RequestIdStateTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the stdio bridge's request-vs-notification detection based on presence and type of the JSON-RPC id.

nodes.name: RequestIdStateTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class asserting request_id_state correctly classifies absent/null/int/string/zero ids.

nodes.name: BoundaryEventEmitterTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests verifying the boundary event emitter drops raw API keys, hashes them to SHA-256, and allowlists tags.

nodes.name: BoundaryEventEmitterTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating tag sanitization and API-key hashing in BoundaryEventEmitter.

nodes.name: ResponseRedactionGateTest.php
nodes.summary: Extensive unit tests for the redaction gate covering exemptions, dash/slash name translation, dual text-channel reconciliation, and cross-bucket safety.

nodes.name: ResponseRedactionGateTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class exhaustively exercising ResponseRedactionGate redaction, exemption scoping, and text-channel regeneration.

nodes.name: CounterStoreTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the counter store covering transient and object-cache backends and fallback behavior.

nodes.name: CounterStoreTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating CounterStore backend selection and increment semantics.

nodes.name: RateLimiterTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the rate limiter covering per-IP/user/site windows, filter overrides, and the separate initialize window.

nodes.name: RateLimiterTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class exercising RateLimiter window enforcement, isolation, and filter hooks.

nodes.name: TrustedProxyResolverTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for trusted-proxy IP resolution covering Cloudflare presets, custom CIDR allowlists, CIDR matching, and IP truncation.

nodes.name: TrustedProxyResolverTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating TrustedProxyResolver header trust logic, CIDR matching, and settings round-trip.

nodes.name: ConfirmationTokenStoreTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the confirmation token store covering minting, one-time consumption, and session/ability/param mismatch rejection.

nodes.name: ConfirmationTokenStoreTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating ConfirmationTokenStore mint/consume and token-binding invariants.

nodes.name: SafetySettingsRepositoryTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the safety settings repository covering keyword bucket CRUD, default overrides, exemptions, and trusted-proxy persistence.

nodes.name: SafetySettingsRepositoryTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating SafetySettingsRepository keyword/exemption/proxy read-write behavior and sanitization.

nodes.name: HttpTransportTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for HttpTransport covering IP-truncation-for-logging and auth-deny enum distinctness.

nodes.name: HttpTransportTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class validating HttpTransport IP truncation and auth-deny constants.

nodes.name: OriginValidatorTest.php
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the origin validator covering same-origin/loopback allowance, allowlist filters, and rejection of evil/subdomain/wildcard origins.

nodes.name: OriginValidatorTest
nodes.summary: PHPUnit test class exhaustively validating OriginValidator allow/deny and echoable-origin behavior.

nodes.name: SettingsAbilities.php
nodes.summary: Registers MCP abilities that let clients inspect and mutate the response-redaction safety configuration (keyword buckets, defaults, ability exemptions) with friction-gated guards.
nodes.languageNotes: WordPress Abilities API registration pattern: paired register_*/execute_* methods per ability.

nodes.name: SettingsAbilities
nodes.summary: Registers and executes the redaction-settings abilities, enforcing Bucket-1 keyword guards, known-ability checks, and confirmation-token friction for risky weakening operations.

nodes.name: SafetyTab.php
nodes.summary: Renders and processes the wp-admin Safety settings tab UI for managing redaction keyword buckets, ability exemptions, and trusted-proxy configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: WordPress admin settings tab with render_*/handle_* method pairs and nonce-guarded form handlers.

nodes.name: SafetyTab
nodes.summary: Admin tab controller that renders the safety settings form sections and handles POST actions (master toggle, keyword add/remove, exemptions, trusted proxy) against the settings repository.

nodes.name: StdioServerBridge.php
nodes.summary: Bridges stdin/stdout JSON-RPC framing to the MCP server for CLI/stdio transport, applying response redaction and producing JSON-RPC error/result envelopes.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes JSON-RPC notifications from requests by presence of the id key (request_id_state).

nodes.name: StdioServerBridge
nodes.summary: Stdio transport bridge that reads JSON-RPC requests from stdin, routes them through the request router with redaction, and writes framed responses to stdout.

nodes.name: BoundaryEventEmitter.php
nodes.summary: Emits sanitized observability events at trust boundaries, dropping raw secrets and replacing API keys with SHA-256 hashes before handing tags to an observability handler.
nodes.languageNotes: Tag allowlisting plus SHA-256 hashing of secrets is a privacy-by-default telemetry pattern.

nodes.name: BoundaryEventEmitter
nodes.summary: Static event emitter that sanitizes a tag allowlist (hashing API keys) and dispatches the event to a configured observability handler.

nodes.name: ConsoleObservabilityHandler.php
nodes.summary: Default observability handler that writes structured boundary events to the PHP error log / console.

nodes.name: ConsoleObservabilityHandler
nodes.summary: Implements the observability handler contract by formatting and writing events to the console/error log.

nodes.name: McpObservabilityHandlerInterface.php
nodes.summary: Defines the contract for observability handlers that record MCP boundary events with tags and timing.

nodes.name: McpObservabilityHandlerInterface
nodes.summary: Interface declaring record_event for pluggable observability backends.

nodes.name: ResponseRedactionGate.php
nodes.summary: Central gate that redacts sensitive content from MCP tool responses, reconciling structured and text channels and honoring per-ability Bucket-3 exemptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Static name-translation cache maps dash-form tool names to slash-form ability names for exemption lookup.

nodes.name: ResponseRedactionGate
nodes.summary: Applies keyword-bucket redaction to tools/call responses, translates dash/slash ability names, reconciles dual text channels, and emits redaction events.

nodes.name: CounterStore.php
nodes.summary: Atomic counter store for rate limiting, backed by the object cache when available and falling back to WordPress transients.

nodes.name: CounterStore
nodes.summary: Increment/get counter store that auto-detects an external object cache backend and falls back to transients with TTL.

nodes.name: RateLimiter.php
nodes.summary: Sliding-window rate limiter enforcing per-IP, per-user, and per-site request quotas with filterable limits and a separate initialize window.

nodes.name: RateLimiter
nodes.summary: Computes and enforces rate-limit windows (IP, user, site, initialize) against the counter store, with WordPress filter hooks for overrides and retry-after calculation.

nodes.name: TrustedProxyResolver.php
nodes.summary: Resolves the real client IP behind trusted proxies (Cloudflare presets or custom CIDR allowlists), validating XFF/CF headers only from trusted sources.
nodes.languageNotes: CIDR matching and IPv6 expansion guard against spoofed X-Forwarded-For from untrusted sources.

nodes.name: TrustedProxyResolver
nodes.summary: Resolves client IP from forwarded headers when the remote address is in a trusted CIDR allowlist, manages Cloudflare IP refresh, and validates CIDR/IPv6 ranges.

nodes.name: ConfirmationTokenStore.php
nodes.summary: Mints and consumes one-time confirmation tokens that gate risky settings mutations, binding each token to session, ability, and parameter hash.

nodes.name: ConfirmationTokenStore
nodes.summary: Stores single-use confirmation tokens keyed to session/ability/params hash, validating and invalidating them on consume.

nodes.name: SafetySettingsRepository.php
nodes.summary: Persistence repository for safety/redaction settings: keyword buckets (1-3), custom and removed defaults, ability exemptions, and trusted-proxy configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Three-tier keyword bucket model with default+custom+removed override layering computed in get_active_keywords.

nodes.name: SafetySettingsRepository
nodes.summary: Reads and writes redaction safety settings to WordPress options, exposing hardcoded default keyword buckets, custom/removed overrides, exemptions, and trusted-proxy fields with sanitization.

nodes.name: McpRestTransportInterface.php
nodes.summary: Defines the contract for REST-based MCP transports (permission check and request handling).

nodes.name: McpRestTransportInterface
nodes.summary: Interface declaring check_permission and handle_request for REST MCP transports.

nodes.name: HttpTransport.php
nodes.summary: HTTP/REST transport that registers MCP routes, enforces authentication, origin checks, and CORS, and delegates request handling to the infrastructure handler.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements McpRestTransportInterface; disables core CORS for MCP routes to emit its own origin-validated headers.

nodes.name: HttpTransport
nodes.summary: Registers WordPress REST routes for MCP, performs permission/origin/CORS checks, handles preflight, and dispatches requests through the HTTP request handler.

nodes.name: HttpRequestContext.php
nodes.summary: Immutable value object capturing per-request HTTP context (the WP_REST_Request and derived fields) passed through the transport pipeline.

nodes.name: HttpRequestContext
nodes.summary: Constructor-built request context holding the REST request and derived transport state.

nodes.name: HttpRequestHandler.php
nodes.summary: Processes MCP JSON-RPC messages over HTTP, handling single/batch requests, SSE, session lifecycle, and transport error emission with redaction.

nodes.name: HttpRequestHandler
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP MCP requests by decoding JSON-RPC messages, processing single/batch/SSE flows, managing session headers, and emitting transport/session observability events.

nodes.name: AuthorizationCodeStore.php
nodes.summary: Stores and consumes OAuth 2.1 authorization codes with PKCE (S256) verification, persisting hashed codes and validating code_verifier on single-use exchange.
nodes.languageNotes: Static store class backed by $wpdb; hashes codes at rest and uses hash_equals for constant-time comparison.

nodes.name: AuthorizationCodeStore
nodes.summary: Static store for OAuth authorization codes with PKCE S256 challenge computation and single-use consumption.

nodes.name: store
nodes.summary: Persists a hashed authorization code with client, user, redirect_uri, scope, PKCE challenge, TTL, and selected role.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Single-use consumption of an authorization code, verifying client, redirect_uri, and PKCE code_verifier before marking it used.

nodes.name: ClientRegistry.php
nodes.summary: Manages dynamically-registered OAuth client records: registration, lookup, redirect_uri validation (loopback/exact match), revocation, and active-client listing.

nodes.name: ClientRegistry
nodes.summary: Static registry of OAuth clients handling registration, lookup, redirect_uri validation, and revocation.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Creates a new OAuth client record with generated id, redirect URIs, scope, and software metadata.

nodes.name: find
nodes.summary: Looks up an active OAuth client by client_id.

nodes.name: redirect_uri_valid
nodes.summary: Validates a requested redirect URI against a client's registered URIs, handling loopback host/port flexibility and exact non-loopback matching.

nodes.name: revoke
nodes.summary: Revokes a client and cascades revocation to its codes and tokens.

nodes.name: list_active
nodes.summary: Returns a paginated list of active OAuth clients.

nodes.name: AuthorizeRequestValidator.php
nodes.summary: Validates incoming OAuth authorize requests, distinguishing pre-redirect errors from redirectable errors and enforcing client, redirect_uri, state, PKCE, scope, and resource constraints.

nodes.name: AuthorizeValidationResult
nodes.summary: Value object representing the outcome of authorize-request validation (ok, pre-redirect error, or redirectable error).

nodes.name: AuthorizeRequestValidator
nodes.summary: Validates OAuth authorize requests and builds typed validation results separating pre-redirect from redirectable errors.

nodes.name: validate

nodes.name: build_error_html

nodes.name: LastConsentLookup
nodes.summary: Records and reads the last interactive consent timestamp per client/user pair and computes elapsed days.

nodes.name: timestamp_for
nodes.summary: Reads the stored last-consent unix timestamp for a client/user pair, returning null when absent or invalid.

nodes.name: days_since
nodes.summary: Returns the integer number of days since the last interactive consent for a client/user pair.

nodes.name: PolicyStore.php
nodes.summary: Resolves the configurable silent-consent maximum-days policy from option and filter, clamped to safe bounds.

nodes.name: PolicyStore
nodes.summary: Reads the consent silent-cap policy (option then filter override) and clamps it within a fixed min/max range.

nodes.name: consent_max_silent_days
nodes.summary: Returns the effective max silent-consent days, applying the option value, filter override, and clamping.

nodes.name: PriorGrantLookup

nodes.name: scopes_for

nodes.summary: Mints transient-backed nonces over the rendered scope set and request context, and validates submitted scope subsets against them.

nodes.name: redeem
nodes.summary: Atomically reads and deletes a stored scope nonce, enforcing single-use redemption of the rendered-scope binding.

nodes.name: submitted_subset_is_valid

nodes.summary: Routes the request to GET or POST handlers based on HTTP method, rejecting others.

nodes.name: handle_post
nodes.summary: Processes the submitted consent form: validates request, redeems the scope nonce, checks role, then mints the code.

nodes.name: submitted_scopes

nodes.name: redirect_with_code
nodes.summary: Issues a redirect to the client redirect_uri carrying the authorization code and state.

nodes.name: redirect_with_error

nodes.name: AuthHeaderProbe

nodes.name: AuthorizationServer

nodes.summary: Runs pattern matchers (password hashes, known API keys, Luhn-valid numbers) against a scalar string and redacts to a marker on a hit.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start at the README to learn what this project is: an adapter that exposes WordPress's Abilities as Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, resources, and prompts so AI clients can discover and call them safely. It introduces the headline features you'll meet throughout the tour: discovery/execution meta-tools, an OAuth 2.1 auth boundary, the admin UI, the four-layer permissions model, and the redaction safety surface. Read this first to get the vocabulary before any code.

tour.title: Architecture & Principles
tour.description: Before reading code, absorb the two documents that define the system's shape. docs/architecture.md describes where the adapter sits in the stack, the directory layout, the request flow, and the security model — essentially a textual version of the tour you're taking. PRINCIPLES.md is the binding architectural contract (registry-first, native WordPress APIs, adapter-as-projection, layered permissions) that every layer ahead is required to honor.

tour.title: Plugin Bootstrap
tour.description: This is depth-0 of the dependency graph and where execution actually begins. abilities-mcp-adapter.php is the WordPress plugin entry file: it defines plugin headers/constants, wires the PSR-4 autoloader, and hooks the core services into WordPress. It hands off to src/Plugin.php, a guarded singleton orchestrator that checks runtime dependencies and boots the MCP adapter plus the admin pages. composer.json shows the package contract behind it all — the PSR-4 mapping of WickedEvolutions\McpAdapter\ to src/ and the PHP >=8.2 requirement.
tour.languageLesson: Plugin.php uses the PHP singleton pattern and blocks __clone() and __wakeup() (unserialization) to guarantee exactly one orchestrator instance — a common hardening technique for WordPress plugin lifecycle objects.

tour.title: MCP Core: Adapter & Server
tour.description: Building on the bootstrap from Step 3, the server-core layer takes over. McpAdapter is the central singleton that wires WordPress hooks, creates MCP servers from configuration, registers the default abilities, and exposes the WP-CLI command. McpServer is the aggregate it produces: it holds the server's identity/config and wires up the component registry, transport factory, and the per-method handlers. These two are the highest-fan-out, highest-fan-in nodes in the core — almost everything downstream flows through them.

tour.title: Component Registry & Server Factory
tour.description: How do WordPress abilities actually become MCP components? McpComponentRegistry is the converter: it turns each ability into an MCP tool, resource, or prompt, validates it, and records observability events on success and failure. DefaultServerFactory is the assembler invoked during boot — it discovers public abilities by type and builds the default server through McpAdapter and McpServerConfig. Together they are the 'projection' machinery the PRINCIPLES contract from Step 2 describes.

tour.title: Domain Value Objects
tour.description: At the heart of the domain layer sit three immutable value objects that model the MCP protocol's primitives. McpTool carries input/output schemas, annotations, and metadata; McpResource models a text or blob resource with URI and mime type; McpPrompt represents a prompt with arguments and an optional builder for direct execution. Each supports serialization, hydration, and validation against the server — they are the shared vocabulary every handler and transport speaks.

tour.title: Ability Adapters & Meta-Tools
tour.description: This step shows the projection from Step 5 in concrete form. RegisterAbilityAsMcpTool is the adapter that builds an McpTool (Step 6) from a raw WordPress ability, transforming its schemas and mapping annotations. The two meta-tools are the product's signature feature: DiscoverAbilitiesAbility lists, filters, and summarizes the abilities exposed over MCP, while ExecuteAbilityAbility runs a single named ability — enforcing permission checks, MCP exposure rules, and OAuth scope before it ever delegates.

tour.title: Request Handlers
tour.description: Once a request reaches the server, per-method handlers process it. InitializeHandler negotiates the MCP protocol version and returns the server's capabilities and identity. ToolsHandler implements tools/list and tools/call — sanitizing tool data, enforcing permission and scope, executing the backing ability, and shaping the result into MCP content. The shared HandlerHelperTrait gives every handler consistent helpers for params and for building success/error envelopes. The graph's tightest cluster forms right here, around these handlers and the server.
tour.languageLesson: HandlerHelperTrait is a PHP trait — horizontal code reuse that lets unrelated handler classes share the same param-extraction and response-building methods without inheritance, avoiding a deep class hierarchy.

tour.title: Transports: HTTP & stdio
tour.description: Handlers don't talk to the network directly — transports do. HttpTransport registers the MCP REST routes and enforces authentication, origin checks, and CORS before delegating to the infrastructure handler. StdioServerBridge offers the same server over stdin/stdout JSON-RPC framing for CLI/stdio clients, applying response redaction on the way out. Both are wired through McpTransportContext, the immutable bundle of server, handlers, observability/error handlers, router, and permission callback — the object that appears in nearly every core cluster.

tour.title: OAuth 2.1 Authorization & Scopes
tour.description: The HTTP transport's authentication from Step 9 is backed by a full OAuth 2.1 boundary. AuthorizationServer boots the OAuth hooks, runs DB migrations, intercepts discovery/authorize routes, registers REST endpoints, and authenticates Bearer tokens narrowed to the MCP resource. ScopeRegistry is the canonical catalog of scopes with umbrella-to-scope expansions and sensitivity flags, while OAuthScopeEnforcer is the gate that derives the required scope for an ability and checks it against what was granted — the enforcement point ExecuteAbilityAbility (Step 7) relies on.

tour.title: OAuth Consent Flow
tour.description: This step follows the human-in-the-loop side of the auth boundary. AuthorizeEndpoint orchestrates the OAuth authorization request: on GET it validates the request, evaluates whether prior consent allows auto-approval, and otherwise renders a consent screen; on POST it verifies the nonce, mints an authorization code, and redirects. ConsentScreenRenderer produces that screen as escaped HTML with grouped, toggleable scope checkboxes, mapping the scopes from Step 10 into a UI the resource owner can review.

tour.title: Infrastructure: Errors, Redaction, Safety
tour.description: Cross-cutting infrastructure keeps every layer above consistent and safe. McpErrorFactory produces standardized JSON-RPC/MCP error responses and maps error codes to HTTP statuses (it has one of the highest fan-ins in the codebase), while McpErrorMapper translates WordPress WP_Error codes into that format. ResponseRedactionGate strips sensitive content from tool responses, driven by configurable keyword buckets and exemptions, and BoundaryEventEmitter emits observability events with secrets hashed out. SECURITY.md documents the threat surface these pieces defend.

tour.title: Admin UI & Permissions
tour.description: Site administrators control the adapter through the WordPress admin UI. AdapterAdminPage is the controller that registers the top-level menu, resolves and renders the Abilities / Connected Bridges / Safety tabs, and dispatches their form submissions. PermissionManager backs the permissions model the README promised: it derives an ability's effective read/write/delete permission from meta or MCP annotations and persists per-ability enabled/disabled settings — the same gate enforced at execution time in Steps 7 and 10, now made configurable. SafetySettingsRepository is the persistence behind the Safety tab this page renders, storing the keyword buckets and exemptions that feed Step 12's redaction gate.

tour.title: Tests & CI Capstone
tour.description: The tour ends where quality is enforced. tests/bootstrap.php stubs WordPress core functions (escaping, options, transients, hooks, REST) so the plugin's classes can be unit-tested without a full WordPress runtime — the trick that makes the large PHPUnit suite fast. phpunit.xml wires that bootstrap, defines the Unit suite, and turns on coverage over src/. The test.yml GitHub Actions workflow runs the whole suite via Composer across a PHP 8.2/8.3 matrix on every push and PR to main, closing the loop on every layer you've just toured.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions matrix builds (here PHP 8.2 and 8.3) run the same job in parallel across each version, catching version-specific regressions before merge. The 'on' triggers (push/pull_request to main) decide when the workflow fires.
